C[Boidhre: cool, I've opened it, I'll read it later :)

C[Himiko: don`t worry about winning or losing, let`s concentrate on helping you improve
Himiko: to do that, I`ll ask you questions while we play
Himiko: so, first of all, why did yo make that last move?
Boidhre: I like it with Q10 and an otherwise ""empty"" board
Himiko: okay, with Q10 in place it is not bad
Boidhre: it's a bit more aggressive than O16 and similar and can lead to some interesting fighting
Himiko: but the important thing is the reason you played there
C[Himiko: If you think it defended territory in the corner, that was mistaken
Boidhre: I learned that many, many stones ago :D
Himiko: But if your idea is to attack R14 with the help of Q10, then it is good
C[Himiko: That move of mine is a bit too slow if I were playing to win
Boidhre: I expected R6 or Q7 or similar
Himiko: yes, that would be faster and more aggressive
C[Himiko: okay, now I have a base (a bit on the small side) and a path to the centre
Himiko: so it is reasonable for me to play elsewhere now
Himiko: Recently I have come to the conclusion that a strong move has Four Qualities
Himiko: 1) It is reasonable 2) it is solid (not leaving bad aji) 3) it is active (builds territory or attacks) 4) has a follow-up
C[Himiko: I think maybe the hardest of these is to be reasonable :-)
C[Boidhre: being reasonable in a large handicap game is...
C[Boidhre: well, possible for pros somehow but not for mortals :)
Himiko: giving handicap can be good training for white...it`s basically a lost position, and you have to be very patient to turn it around
C[Himiko: okay, why did you choose that move?
Boidhre: a few thoughts behind it
Himiko: I`m not saying if I think it`s good or bad :-)
Boidhre: first it makes connection more difficult for you
Himiko: right, it prevents my connection
Boidhre: if you connect on the side you're basically alive and this is too good for you
Boidhre: it has a follow up jump underneath your stones, not for now but it might be nasty later
Himiko: yes, it would be hard for me to get eyes around S6
Boidhre: and it removes a few ""shenanigans"" from using the cut in the knight jump for tempo
Himiko: can you think of any drawbacks to black`s choice?
Boidhre: that's more of a connection thing
Boidhre: I'm behind on top in the race
Boidhre: it doesn't immediately threaten either group
Himiko: basically, I think it`s a very strong move but
Himiko: you`ve essentially nailed your colours to the mast
Boidhre: yes, I need to get an attack going
Boidhre: otherwise this move is pointless
Himiko: an alternative move might have been simply to play another very large but peaceful move, such as D6
Himiko: if you can make this attack work, it could result in a spectacular win
Boidhre: if you connect underneath, which you can, I end up with a very weak floating group in the centre
C[Himiko: okay, well you`ve said the fighting words now :-)
Boidhre: also, I do kinda use high handicap games as fighting practice :)
Boidhre: I'm too peaceful in even games I think
C[Himiko: I`m starting to think it`s not so much that one is too peaceful or too aggressive, but rather that what counts is realising what the specific position calls for
Boidhre: yes, that is what I mean
Himiko: some positions call for profit-taking, others must be fought
Boidhre: I too often take the peaceful route when it is not the correct choice
C[Himiko: taking territory is good, but not merely clinging to it
Himiko: that is what I mean by the third quality of a good move - it is active
C[Himiko: put it this way, even in the endgame a single move can be worth 20 or more points (!), so you *have* to be active
Boidhre: You can still push out, I can't stop you :)
Himiko: my concern is that if I play something based on N15 I might damage myself more than you
C[Boidhre: I need to go afk for a few minutes, brb
C[Himiko: no, I think it was a very good move
C[Boidhre: I was thinking I could get Q17 in first
Himiko: N17 is good, restricting my living space, and also limits the aji of O14 a lot
C[Himiko: for instance, I can`t do anything with N15 now
Boidhre: I don't think I can kill cleanly here
Boidhre: but I can make you live in gote :)
Himiko: yes, to make the other live small and in gote is a great result
C[Himiko: okay, why did you play there?
Boidhre: I want to put some pressure on the bottom group but my main aim is harassing the three stones in the centre, I'd also like to get M3 or similar in
Himiko: I think it`s very strong
C[Boidhre: I'm not 100% sure this works, not looking for a kill but S10 in sente
C[Himiko: it seems to be an overplay
Himiko: yes, I think it would have made it mandatory to try to live with R11, which I don`t know was possible, and would have put Q6 on the spot too
Himiko: I think it is joseki
Himiko: breakfast showed me this move ages ago
Himiko: never was able to remember how to play it properly though
Boidhre: oh dear, I'm discovering joseki moves now...
Boidhre: I need to be embarrassed by my rank now! :P
C[Himiko: that`s quite the moyo developing in the centre
Himiko: now, the usual response people have at our level is to try to jump in and destroy it all
Himiko: but they say that ""keshi is worth as much as an invasion""
Boidhre: you lose if you fail :)
Boidhre: on the otherhand if you can kill something big...
C[Himiko: okay, you defend one threat, but now I can carry out another
Boidhre: this happens a lot with strong sdks/low dan players :P
Himiko: perhaps that is one meaning of keshi worth as much as invasion
C[Boidhre: afk briefly, my daughter has woken
Himiko: Maybe D3 was too big to tenuki
C[Himiko: the idea of D3 was to clarify the situation in the lower left
Boidhre: D18 removes a lot of aji from the moyo
Boidhre: and makes B14 sente for me
Himiko: if I had just jumped to J6 straightaway, you might have played E3 and taken a lot of points that I could not contest
C[Himiko: D18 is an endgame move
C[Boidhre: I thought I saw something interesting with a deep invasion and it
C[Boidhre: I'll need to study it later, I may have been imagining things :)
Himiko: okay :-) I agree with a lot of your reasoning, but it seems to me that D3 was a more urgent area

C[Boidhre: well, my main idea was if I get the moyo I can win, D18 helps in two ways, removing some aji on top and giving me B14 in sente which makes invading the left hand side more annoying though far from impossible
Boidhre: the way I see it right now I can get something nice on the left or the centre when you push up from L6
C[Himiko: yes, at some stage I will have to figure out how to keep the centre from growing too big
C[Himiko: my internet connection is really intermittent today
C[Boidhre: H6 is messy for me
Boidhre: mine's been acting up too lately
Boidhre: yes, it was a tough choice :)
Himiko: I`d have gone for H6
Himiko: my next move is going to hurt you
C[Himiko: this is not a ko fight that you`re going to enjoy :-)
C[Himiko: the reason I would go for H6 instead of F5 is the same that I objected to D18 - it`s about priorities
Himiko: in this position I have to let you have something, and I am thinking the that the left side is smaller
Boidhre: my middlegame judgement sucks pretty badly :)
Himiko: you`ve only been playing for how long? 18 months?
Boidhre: I technically started last March
Boidhre: but I spent over 6 months depressed or manic and not playing
Boidhre: and since the start of the year I've played maybe 10 games
C[Himiko: well, I reckon you have already learned a lot
Boidhre: I seem to pick it up fairly quickly, I'm not sure why
C[Himiko: well, I wouldn`t worry about it - just be happy!
C[Himiko: did you think about playing the ko?
Boidhre: I saw far fewer threats for me of sufficient size
Boidhre: I'm not good at counting threats though
Himiko: because my H9 was a bit stretched, it would have been a difficult fight for me too
C[Himiko: one way I like to think about it is that attacking, when you have a chance, is a duty
C[Himiko: defending where necessary is a duty that is easy to understand, but likewise, a good soldier has to attack when they get a clear chance
C[Himiko: my last move was a misread
Himiko: G9 would have been difficult for e
C[Himiko: no choice for me there
C[Boidhre: oh that was a horrible misread by me :D
Himiko: that looks a bit unreasonable
Himiko: is it possible to undo?
C[Boidhre: usually you can't on correspondance servers
C[Boidhre: hmm C12 was better for me
C[Boidhre: so many things need fixing :/
C[Boidhre: damn kids, mixed up my move order :/
Himiko: patience is important - I did not dive in trying to destroy it all
Boidhre: as an EGF 2k friend of mine says, the secret is waiting for the mistake :)
Himiko: well, maybe a nicer way to put it is to wait for your chance
Himiko: but, yes, at our level, it is not as though the opponent is going to play flawlessly :-)
Himiko: the key is not to go crazy while waiting for that chance
C[Boidhre: let's have a ko fight :)
C[Boidhre: I don't see the threat :S
C[Himiko: B5 in sente is very big, and I was very pleased to get it
Himiko: it`s been there a long time, but not big enough
C[Himiko: still, it may well be that you have enough points to win
C[Himiko: it`s probably going to come down to the ko on Q12
Himiko: the ko is quite heavy for me too
C[Himiko: ah, if I had half a brain I`d have played M15
Himiko: I only get one threat this way
C[Boidhre: very heavy for me now :D
C[Himiko: it`s too big to let you capture those stones, and I don`t have so many threats either
Boidhre: yeah those stones were the source of most of your threats earlier
Himiko: best to let those guys go
C[Boidhre: yeah just working out the best way
Boidhre: it didn't score the top
Boidhre: or two points for you in the centre
Himiko: well, it is your victory.
Himiko: The bug in the server is not our fault
Boidhre: yes, we should report it
Himiko: and it was an unranked game, so it does not count to your rank anyway
Boidhre: oh I don't mind at all
Boidhre: more I hate seeing scoring bugs
Himiko: I think you played very well, and that you have a good idea about strategy
Boidhre: my reading is letting me down
Boidhre: just not playing often enough
Himiko: it`s only a matter of playing a lot of games and doing life and death and other puzzles
Himiko: in fact, I`m going to go out soon to buy a book of tactical puzzles I think
Himiko: okay, well, I have to eat something and get ready to go out
Boidhre: I think e18 screwed up the machine
Himiko: I was impressed by your play
Himiko: thank you for the game

C[Boidhre: A more standard reply (from what I've seen played) is e3 here. With various continuations.
Another reply is d3 or f3, both of which prevent me playing e3. The reply here to d3 is f3 and vice versa. Again, from what I've seen not what I know.
C[Boidhre: This move invites c5 to split your stones apart. Having your stones split apart is bad.
C[Mambi: This move i want to ask you about later..
C[Mambi: i was going to ask if i showed have moved where you did, E7.., but thought a corner is more important..
C[Mambi: That's what I thought after the fact.  This is my last move for the night, we chat tomorrow.  Again, Thank you!!
C[Boidhre: I don't have to answer this when I can start killing c3 and c7.
Boidhre: Reducing potential eye space for c7.
C[Boidhre: Needed to be at B4 for a chance at life. Even still it's possible to die here if you play B4. It depends on you and your opponent's life and death skills which is always a gamble.
C[Boidhre: This is the vital point for this shape. It's rectangular six in the corner: http://senseis.xmp.net/?RectangularSixInTheCorner

It's complicated here because there's a ko. You can't win this ko fight though as you lack any threat remotely close to the value of the entire corner plus the stones in it, so the corner is effectively dead.
C[Mambi: the way i rread it, that group is dead.. i did not recgonize a shape though..
C[Mambi: thank you i really appreciate it!
C[Mambi: you took away the eye space right?, plus that corner is caught in a net, i think,, just stuff i want to go over after the game..
Boidhre: Standard killing move for straight three.

C[topazg: Good timing. Rule of thumb - if the push at F3 and cut doesn't work, you jump ahead. Staying ahead is one of the basic fundamentals to always look for.

C[topazg: The only reason I can see to play E9 is to then respond D8 and cut, relying on a long, complicated fight. Otherwise, something like C12 seems better - using the C7 aji to develop your top left corner.
C[topazg: White is pretty satisfied here. The 3 stones are floating, so White has gained good profit in sente.
C[topazg: Very important, and good placement.
C[topazg: The only move I think - something has to be done to negate Black's new wall.

C[topazg: Too slow. You have to pincer and work towards a whole game plan. If I create a settled group here, not only do you get few corner points with C12 staring into it, but White can develop points on top and influence facing the H12 area to contest Black's presence there.
C[topazg: So, so slow. R14 works nicely, G4 is probably fine too, building thickness along the lower edge.
C[topazg: I think this makes sense. You may want to pincer tightly at R8 for sente, but I can create a fight more easily that way.
C[topazg: Very happy to get a sente corner for this point. White is ahead now.
C[topazg: Black's wall makes the F17 - J16 a bit weak. I wanted to reinforce and generate thickness. White has done well enough on points so far to drop a corner for long term security.
C[topazg: You can try S14 here, this feels slow.
C[topazg: N5 more normal as it prevents the bend being so severe.
C[topazg: Good direction, but too slow. G15 has enough threats that you should be able to follow with J14, developing points in the middle.

C[topazg: Continuing at H14 looks very big. Even if you connect to P14, the wall's value is diminished by my getting securely into the middle. You're better off making the beginnings of real points - certainly powerful influence - and threating doing something from this side as well.
C[topazg: By this point, Black has very little scope for points, and White is comfortably ahead on the board.
C[topazg: You can also consider N6 - your shape isn't at risk with that group, so playing to build the middle is valuable.
C[topazg: Trying to maximise profits and reduce the value of P11 - N6 thickness.
C[topazg: G4 is normal (G4->H5->J4->K5->M6 and you have a nice wall).
C[topazg: Big, and important. I need to reduce before responding.
C[topazg: Maybe call me on this one. Play M13 and force me to fight - why not? :)
C[topazg: Overplay. I forgot about B5.
C[topazg: Take B5 in sente first.
C[topazg: Important area to reduce, but it's not big enough.
C[topazg: Maybe go crazy with L12 and try to kill the stones? Any plan that could pick up a lot of points is worth trying at this stage.
C[topazg: Many thanks for the game :)
